text.title: Serverless Architecture Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 8 testable rules grounding the serverless-architecture-patterns methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Pattern selection MUST start from invocation pattern: sync HTTP → API Gateway + Lambda or containers; async event → EventBridge / SQS + Lambda; workflow > 15min → Step Functions or containers; high fan-out → SQS / SNS + Lambda.

rationale.source: AWS Serverless lens; cloud-provider reference architectures.

rule.rationale: Mismatched composition produces 10× cost or timeout failures.

rule.statement: Cost MUST be modelled at expected RPS using vendor pricing (per-invocation + per-GB-sec + per-request downstream). The break-even RPS vs reserved containers MUST be named.

rationale.source: AWS / GCP serverless pricing case studies.

rule.rationale: Above the break-even RPS (typically ~50-150 RPS for steady traffic), reserved compute wins on cost; cost-blind serverless picks burn budget.

rule.statement: If latency budget is below 200ms p99, cold-start tolerance MUST be assessed; either provisioned concurrency, SnapStart, or container-based alternative MUST be chosen.

rationale.source: AWS Lambda SnapStart docs; cold-start benchmark studies.

rule.rationale: Cold starts can blow a tight latency budget by 1-3 seconds; ignoring this produces SLO misses post-launch.

rule.statement: Every async-triggered Lambda MUST be idempotent: design for at-least-once delivery; key the operation by event id; persist a dedup record (DynamoDB / Redis) before side effects.
rule.rationale: EventBridge / SQS / S3 events all deliver at-least-once; non-idempotent handlers create duplicate side effects.

rule.statement: Spec MUST include X-Ray / OpenTelemetry tracing wired through API Gateway → Lambda → downstream services; logs MUST include the correlation id.
rule.rationale: Serverless decomposition fragments call paths; without traces, debugging incident root cause becomes guesswork.

rule.statement: Pattern selection MUST include an IaC template (SAM / Serverless Framework / CDK / Pulumi); console-clicked stacks MUST NOT be approved.
rule.rationale: Console state drifts; IaC is the only way to reproduce + audit serverless topology.

rule.statement: When prerequisites hold (workload profile, latency budget, exec profile, provider), apply r1 → r6 and emit the ADR.
rule.rationale: Order encodes risk: invocation pattern first, cost second, cold start third, idempotency fourth, observability fifth, IaC last.

rule.statement: Do NOT use when (a) sustained high RPS, (b) long-running stateful workload, (c) sub-50ms hard requirement. Choose containers and exit.
rule.rationale: Serverless ceremony for these workloads loses on cost or correctness.
